mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in PAYEMS indicates strong labor market conditions that support corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances despite positive PAYEMS trends, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS signals an easing cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of easing periods, or FEDFUNDS declines correlate negatively with SPX returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in RSAFS and HOUST reflects strong consumer demand and housing activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS and HOUST growth correlate negatively with SPX returns (p > 0.05) in 60d windows, or interaction effect Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of INDPRO decline instances during late-cycle periods, or INDPRO declines show no correlation with SPX (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures that force monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of CPIAUCSL > 2.5% instances, or CPIAUCSL shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDPC1 combined with rising UNRATE indicates economic slowdown, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines > 0.5% qoq and UNRATE rises > 0.3pp
failure_condition: GDPC1 and UNRATE signals show no correlation with SPX returns (p > 0.05) in 60d windows, or interaction effect Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in UNRATE below 3.5% signals tight labor markets that boost corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of UNRATE < 3.5% instances, or UNRATE shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in INDPRO above 105 combined with falling HOUST indicates overcapacity in manufacturing and housing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO and HOUST signals show no correlation with SPX returns (p > 0.05) in 20d windows, or interaction effect Rank IC < 0.02.
